# Let's load the text file to analyze
path = '/mnt/data/file-AUdaK8PY2SW2DBRDMG8scH-text_to_analyse.txt'
with open(path, 'r', encoding='utf-8') as f:
    text = f.read()
len(text), text[:5000]

import re, json, textwrap

text = open('/mnt/data/file-AUdaK8PY2SW2DBRDMG8scH-text_to_analyse.txt', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()

# Find all double-quote spans
spans = []
for m in re.finditer(r'"([^"]+)"', text):
    spans.append({
        'span_start': m.start(1),
        'span_end': m.end(1),
        'span_text': m.group(1)
    })
len(spans), spans[:10]

import re

text = open('/mnt/data/file-AUdaK8PY2SW2DBRDMG8scH-text_to_analyse.txt', 'r', encoding='utf-8').read()

# Find the item 7 section of the text to work within
item7_start = text.find('7- حديث سراقة بن مالك')
item7_end = text.find('8- ', item7_start)
item7_text = text[item7_start:item7_end] if item7_end != -1 else text[item7_start:]
item7_start, item7_end, item7_text

segments = []

# find "حيث قال:" and capture until the next "قال:"
hatha_qal = item7_text.find('حيث قال:')
if hatha_qal != -1:
    after = item7_text[hatha_qal + len('حيث قال:'):]
    next_qal = after.find('قال:')
    seg1 = after[:next_qal].strip()
    # compute span positions in the full text
    seg1_start = item7_start + hatha_qal + len('حيث قال:')
    seg1_end = seg1_start + len(seg1)
    segments.append((seg1, seg1_start, seg1_end))

# now find subsequent "قال:" occurrences and capture their segments
cursor_local = item7_start + item7_text.find('قال:')
# We'll iterate through "قال:" instances within item7_text
for m in re.finditer(r'قال:\s*', item7_text):
    start_local = item7_start + m.end()
    # Determine end of this segment: it's before the next "قال:" or "رواه مسلم" or end of item7
    next_m = None
    for n in re.finditer(r'قال:\s*', item7_text[m.end():]):
        next_m = n
        break
    # compute absolute end
    if next_m:
        end_local = item7_start + m.end() + next_m.start()
    else:
        # look for "رواه مسلم"
        rawa = item7_text.find('رواه مسلم', m.end())
        if rawa != -1:
            end_local = item7_start + m.end() + (rawa - m.end())
        else:
            end_local = item7_start + len(item7_text)
    seg_text = text[start_local:end_local].strip()
    segments.append((seg_text, start_local, end_local))

segments

segments = []
i = 0
local = item7_text

# find index of 'حيث قال:'
idx = local.find('حيث قال:')
if idx != -1:
    # First segment: from after idx to next 'قال:'
    start1_local = idx + len('حيث قال:')
    next_qal = local.find('قال:', start1_local)
    seg1_local_end = next_qal if next_qal != -1 else len(local)
    seg1 = local[start1_local:seg1_local_end].strip()
    # absolute indices
    seg1_start = item7_start + start1_local
    seg1_end = item7_start + seg1_local_end
    segments.append((seg1, seg1_start, seg1_end))

    # Now iterate next "قال:" positions starting at next_qal
    pos = next_qal
    while pos != -1:
        start_local = pos + len('قال:')
        # find next 'قال:' after start_local
        next_pos = local.find('قال:', start_local)
        # if none, end at 'رواه مسلم'
        rawa = local.find('رواه مسلم', start_local)
        end_local = None
        if next_pos != -1 and (rawa == -1 or next_pos < rawa):
            end_local = next_pos
        elif rawa != -1:
            end_local = rawa
        else:
            end_local = len(local)
        seg = local[start_local:end_local].strip()
        segments.append((seg, item7_start + start_local, item7_start + end_local))
        pos = next_pos

for s in segments:
    print(repr(s[0]), s[1], s[2])

# Identify the "References" block and search for the particular quotes
ref_start = text.find('References:')
refs_text = text[ref_start:] if ref_start != -1 else ''

# Candidate phrases to search for (as they appear)
candidate_ref_phrases = [
    'الدُّنْيَا نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا وَمَنْ يُرِدْ ثَوَابَ الْآخِرَةِ نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا',
    'نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا وَمَنْ يُرِدْ ثَوَابَ الْآخِرَةِ نُؤْتِهِ مِنْهَا',
    'إِنَّ هَذِهِ تَذْكِرَةٌ فَمَنْ شَاءَ اتَّخَذَ إِلَى رَبِّهِ سَبِيلاً',
    'إن الله خير عبدا بين الدنيا وبين ما عنده، فاختار ما عنده'
]

ref_spans = []
for phrase in candidate_ref_phrases:
    start = 0
    while True:
        idx = text.find(phrase, ref_start + start)
        if idx == -1:
            break
        ref_spans.append((phrase, idx, idx + len(phrase)))
        start = (idx - ref_start) + 1  # move forward

ref_spans

# Combine spans: six major quoted spans + the four segments + reference spans then filter nested duplicates.
main_spans = spans.copy()
# Add item7 segments
for s, st, en in segments:
    main_spans.append({'span_text': s, 'span_start': st, 'span_end': en})

# Add reference spans
for s, st, en in ref_spans:
    main_spans.append({'span_text': s, 'span_start': st, 'span_end': en})

len(main_spans)

print(text.count(phrase1), text.count(phrase2))

# show indices of phrase2
indices = []
start = 0
while True:
    idx = text.find(phrase2, start)
    if idx == -1:
        break
    indices.append(idx)
    start = idx+1
indices

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
